name: Lamb Shoulder in Pastry (Guilainn Chaoireola i Dtaosran)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the pastry and chill for at least 1 hour before using.
2. Ensure the lamb is fairly free of fat, and securely tied. Place in a roasting pan and rub with about one-third of the butter.
3. Put into a very hot oven (450°F) for 20-30 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to cool slightly.
4. Mix the remaining butter with herbs, salt, and pepper.
5. Roll out the pastry to a piece large enough to wrap around the joint.
6. Carefully remove the string from the lamb, trying to retain its shape.
7. Place the lamb in the center of the pastry, dampen the edges, and roll the pastry up over the top. Secure well by squeezing the pastry ends and edges together.
8. Turn the parcel so that the fold is underneath, place on a baking sheet, and prick all over the top lightly with a fork.
9. Brush with milk or beaten egg.
10. Put into a preheated oven (400°F) and cook for about 1/2 hour, or until the pastry is nicely browned. Turn the baking sheet once during cooking to ensure even browning.
11. Serve cut into fairly thick slices.
